nodes.label: Project Skill Trigger Registry
nodes.rationale: Registers the /graphify and /commit triggers so the skills are invoked before anything else when typed.

nodes.label: /commit Skill
nodes.rationale: Encodes the repo's required commit/push workflow so every commit updates the README status and knowledge graph, passes safety checks, and carries a reviewable message.

nodes.label: Project Upkeep Step
nodes.rationale: Required before every push: refresh graphify-out via graphify update when source changed, and update the README Project status so it describes the post-commit state.

nodes.label: Commit Safety Checks
nodes.rationale: Never commit .env/secrets, node_modules, .venv, or build output; .claude/ and graphify-out/ are deliberately committed exceptions and must never be gitignored.

nodes.label: Commit Message Standard
nodes.rationale: A collaborator not in the session must understand the work from the message alone; no filler like 'various fixes'.

nodes.label: /graphify Skill
nodes.rationale: Turns any folder of code, docs, papers, images, or video into a persistent queryable knowledge graph with an honest EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS audit trail and community detection.

nodes.label: Fast Path Query on Existing Graph
nodes.source_location: What You Must Do When Invoked
nodes.rationale: If graphify-out/graph.json exists and the request is a natural-language question, skip the entire build pipeline and run graphify query directly — the graph is already built.
nodes.norm_label: fast path query on existing graph

nodes.label: AST Structural Extraction
nodes.rationale: Deterministic and free: code is extracted structurally with no LLM and no API key, so a code-only corpus skips semantic extraction entirely.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction via Parallel Subagents
nodes.rationale: Docs, papers, and images need LLM extraction; general-purpose subagents dispatched in a single message run in parallel (5-10x faster than sequential reads), and read-only Explore agents silently drop results because they cannot write chunk files.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction Cache
nodes.rationale: Files already extracted are served from cache so only uncached files cost subagent tokens; stale cache files from prior runs are deleted so merges never include them.

nodes.label: Community Detection and Clustering
nodes.rationale: Communities surface cross-document connections you wouldn't think to ask about; each gets a 2-5 word plain-language label in Step 5.

nodes.label: God Nodes
nodes.rationale: Highest-connectivity nodes reported in GRAPH_REPORT.md; also reused as domain hints for Whisper transcription.

nodes.label: Graph Health Check
nodes.rationale: Read-only integrity gate before labeling; surfaces dangling/missing endpoints, self-loops, and edge collapse — the silent-corruption modes of incremental updates and AST/LLM id mismatches — without aborting.

nodes.label: Graph Shrink Guard
nodes.rationale: to_json refuses to write a smaller graph over an existing graph.json (#479) so an empty or failed extraction never clobbers a good graph, report, or analysis sidecar.

nodes.label: Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent an edge (use AMBIGUOUS if unsure); always show token cost; show raw cohesion numbers; warn before visualizing graphs over 5,000 nodes.

nodes.label: Cumulative Cost Tracker
nodes.rationale: cost.json accumulates per-run and all-time token counts so the token cost of graph building is always visible, per the Honesty Rules.

nodes.label: EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS Taxonomy
nodes.rationale: Every edge carries an audit tag so users can distinguish facts explicit in sources from inferences and flagged uncertainties.

nodes.label: Extraction Subagent Prompt
nodes.rationale: The exact verbatim prompt each semantic subagent receives, defining the JSON schema, node-ID rules, confidence rubric, hyperedge and vision rules.

nodes.label: Discrete Confidence Score Rubric
nodes.rationale: Discrete values (0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55) replace a continuous range because production data showed models collapse ranges to a bimodal binary (>50% at 0.5, >40% at 0.85+).

nodes.label: Deterministic Node ID Format
nodes.rationale: Full repo-relative path stem keeps same-named files in different directories distinct and must match the AST extractor's IDs, otherwise orphan ghost-duplicate nodes appear; no chunk suffixes so IDs are deterministic from the label alone.

nodes.label: Query/Path/Explain Flow
nodes.rationale: Answers questions against an existing graph via the graphify query CLI, falling back to an inline NetworkX traversal when the CLI is unavailable; answers use only what the graph contains.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Expansion
nodes.rationale: The query CLI matches by case-folded substring with no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language match, so vocabulary mismatch returns zero hits; expanding the question against the graph's own vocabulary (never inventing tokens) fixes this without fabricating searches.

nodes.label: BFS/DFS Traversal Modes
nodes.rationale: BFS (default) for broad context — what is X connected to; DFS for tracing a specific chain — how does X reach Y. DFS is depth-limited to 6 and BFS to 3 layers to avoid traversing the whole graph.

nodes.label: save-result Feedback Loop
nodes.rationale: Answers are saved back into the graph with outcome tags (useful/dead_end/corrected) so the next --update extracts each Q&A as a node — a self-improving loop that also records the vocab-expansion trace.

nodes.label: Work Memory (LESSONS.md Reflections)
nodes.rationale: graphify reflect distills prior outcomes into preferred sources, known dead ends, and corrections read at session start; --if-stale makes the refresh nearly free when the git hook already updated it.

nodes.label: Incremental Update (--update)
nodes.rationale: Only re-extracts new or changed files to save tokens and time; code-only changes skip semantic extraction entirely (no LLM), and changed video files are transcribed before being fed to subagents.

nodes.label: build_merge Replace-on-Re-extract
nodes.rationale: Reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction is preserved (#801); prune_sources is only for genuinely deleted files since replace-on-re-extract reconciles changed files (#1344), and root= relativizes paths so pruning actually matches (#1361).

nodes.label: Cluster-only Rebuild
nodes.rationale: Self-contained recluster of the existing graph regenerating report, JSON, and HTML; must not re-run pipeline Steps 5-9 because their intermediate files were deleted by a prior cleanup (#1392).

nodes.label: Whisper Video/Audio Transcription
nodes.rationale: Video/audio is transcribed to text first and the transcripts treated as docs; the host model composes a domain-hint initial prompt from god-node labels itself, avoiding a separate API call. JSON is written from Python, not shell redirect, because Whisper prints progress to stdout.

nodes.label: URL Ingestion (/graphify add)
nodes.rationale: Auto-detects YouTube/Twitter/arXiv/PDF/image/webpage URL types, saves to ./raw with optional author/contributor tags, then runs the --update pipeline to merge into the existing graph.

nodes.label: Watch Mode Auto-rebuild
nodes.rationale: Code-only changes rebuild via AST immediately with no LLM; doc/paper/image changes only write a needs_update flag because semantic re-extraction requires an LLM. Debounce (3s) prevents one rebuild per file during parallel agent write waves.

nodes.label: MCP Graph Server
nodes.rationale: stdio MCP server exposing query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, get_community, god_nodes, graph_stats, shortest_path so other agents can query the graph live; Claude Desktop config must use the absolute interpreter path because it cannot run shell substitution.

nodes.label: Neo4j/FalkorDB Cypher Export
nodes.rationale: Uses MERGE so re-runs are idempotent; FalkorDB prefers direct push because GRAPH.QUERY runs one statement at a time with no bulk script import like Neo4j's cypher-shell.

nodes.label: Token Reduction Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Only run when total_words > 5,000; for small corpora the graph's value is structural clarity, not token compression.

nodes.label: GitHub Clone Flow
nodes.rationale: Clones into ~/.graphify/repos/<owner>/<repo> and reuses existing clones on repeat runs.

nodes.label: Cross-repo Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: merge-graphs combines per-repo graph.json files with a repo attribute on each node; monorepo subfolders use the CLI per subfolder because the skill pipeline would clobber a single shared graphify-out/.

nodes.label: Post-commit Auto-rebuild Hook
nodes.rationale: Triggers once per commit with no background process; detects changed code files via git diff HEAD~1, re-runs AST on them only, and appends to any existing post-commit hook rather than replacing it. Doc/image changes are ignored — those need a manual --update.

nodes.label: Native CLAUDE.md Integration
nodes.rationale: graphify claude install writes a ## graphify section to the project CLAUDE.md so Claude checks the graph before answering codebase questions and rebuilds it after code changes in every session.

nodes.label: Ominin
nodes.rationale: Side business providing computer and AI solutions to restaurants to reduce operating costs and facilitate day-to-day operations: cost tracking, forecasting, automation of back-office work.

nodes.label: Business Constraints
nodes.rationale: Solo/side project favors simple low-maintenance solutions over clever ones; infrastructure must run on free tiers until revenue justifies upgrades; must scale to 100+ users without re-architecture.

nodes.label: Next.js Frontend
nodes.rationale: Next.js (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind hosted on Vercel free tier; the customer-facing dashboard lives in frontend/.

nodes.label: FastAPI Backend
nodes.rationale: FastAPI + Python 3.13 managed with uv, hosted on Render free tier. Python was chosen because AI/data work (forecasting, invoice OCR, cost analysis) is the core of the product.

nodes.label: Supabase (DB/Auth/Storage)
nodes.rationale: Postgres, auth, and storage on the Supabase free tier (50k free monthly auth users).

nodes.label: Claude API Integration
nodes.rationale: Claude API via the Python SDK, pay-per-use; powers the AI features (forecasting, invoice OCR, cost analysis) served from the backend.

nodes.label: Environment-only Settings Convention
nodes.rationale: Backend settings come only from app/config.py (pydantic-settings) and environment variables loaded from .env; never hardcode secrets or URLs.

nodes.label: CORS Restricted to Frontend Origin
nodes.rationale: CORS is restricted to the frontend origin via the FRONTEND_ORIGIN env var rather than a hardcoded URL.

nodes.label: Code Quality Rules
nodes.rationale: No AI slop: no filler comments, dead code, speculative abstractions, or template boilerplate. Never hardcode constants — placement (config file, .env, or inline) is the user's decision, discussed before introduction.

nodes.label: Push Workflow Rules
nodes.rationale: README Project status is updated on every push as the source of truth for collaborators; graphify update . after code changes is agent discipline, not automation — no hook runs it. Commits go through the /commit skill.

nodes.label: Graph-first Codebase Queries
nodes.rationale: For codebase questions, run graphify query/path/explain first when graph.json exists — they return a scoped subgraph much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep output; the wiki index serves broad navigation.

nodes.label: Project Status Section
nodes.rationale: The living source-of-truth section describing project state after each push; currently records the QR menu page as the first product feature and the committed skills.

nodes.label: QR Menu Page (/m/[slug])
nodes.rationale: First product feature: the customer-facing digital menu guests see after scanning a table QR code, modeled on Miracards' menus with a dark-premium gradient design. Per-restaurant URLs at /m/[slug], mock data at /m/trattoria-lucia, / redirects there; order buttons rendered but disabled until the ordering flow and backend data exist.

nodes.label: LLM Agent Setup Guide
nodes.rationale: Written for an AI coding agent: every setup step has a verification command whose expected output must be confirmed before proceeding, and failures are reported rather than skipped.

nodes.label: graphifyy CLI
nodes.rationale: PyPI package is 'graphifyy' (double y), installed per machine via uv tool or pipx; the skill files themselves are committed in the repo so every collaborator uses the same version.

nodes.label: Next.js Agent Rules
nodes.rationale: Warns that this Next.js version has breaking changes versus model training data; agents must read the guides in node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing any code and heed deprecation notices.

nodes.label: Frontend CLAUDE.md Include
nodes.rationale: Contains only @AGENTS.md — delegates all frontend agent instructions to AGENTS.md.

nodes.label: create-next-app Boilerplate README
nodes.rationale: Stock create-next-app README: dev server instructions, next/font with Geist, and Vercel deployment links.

nodes.label: Vercel Logo SVG (default Next.js template asset)
nodes.norm_label: vercel logo svg (default next.js template asset)
